channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: As Guthrie Case Grips Nation, One Police Chief Reflects on His Time in the Spotlight
item.description: “It’s probably something that no one ever expects to happen in their career,” said James Fry, who was the police chief in Moscow, Idaho, when four stu...

item.title: Police Respond to Shooting at a Rhode Island Hockey Arena
item.description: The authorities said they were responding on Monday to a shooting at the Dennis M. Lynch Arena in Pawtucket, where a high school hockey game was sched...

item.title: In Hungary, Marco Rubio Stresses Trump’s Support for Viktor Orban
item.description: The U.S. secretary of state said in Budapest that the president was “deeply committed” to the success of Viktor Orban, who has boasted of creating an ...

item.title: Amazon’s Ring decides maybe partnering with a police surveillance firm is a bad idea after wide revulsion at Super Bowl ad
item.description: The Super Bowl ad depicts a lost dog being tracked by cameras throughout a neighborhood using artificial intelligence.

item.title: Top Hollywood screenwriter warns TikTok’s new tool is at the gates: ‘I hate to say it. It’s likely over for us’
item.description: Seedance 2.0, which is only available in China for now, lets users generate high-quality AI videos using simple text prompts.

item.title: The ultrawealthy have 3 big secrets on reducing taxes including the way they die
item.description: “Assets like stocks can quietly grow for decades and, when they’re inherited, the tax bill often disappears.”

item.title: Americans’ new tariff coffee math means ditching the Starbucks, McDonald’s and Dunkin’ runs
item.description: "Twenty cents a cup compared to $7 or $8 a cup. The math just makes sense."

item.title: Syrian Army Crosses New Threshold With Advances in Kurdish Regions
item.description: The advance of the Syrian Army into Kurdish regions could put nearly all the country under one authority. But it ends a dream of autonomy for ethnic K...

item.title: Where to Watch Robert Duvall’s Top Performances
item.description: He played rugged, capable men drawn from America’s past, present and possible future.

item.title: Wendy’s closes hundreds of restaurants after plunge in same-store sales worse than Wall Street expected
item.description: "One learning from 2025 around value, we swung the pendulum too far towards limited-time price promotions instead of everyday value."

item.title: Rage. Grief. Anxiety. The New Mood in Iran.
item.description: In the aftermath of another wave of antigovernment unrest, Iran is gripped by a mood of collective grief and uncertainty about the future.

item.title: In Hungary, Marco Rubio Stresses Trump’s Support for Viktor Orban
item.description: The U.S. secretary of state said in Budapest that the president was “deeply committed” to the success of Viktor Orban, who has boasted of creating an ...

item.title: In Xi Jinping’s Purge of the Military, a Search for Absolute Loyalty
item.description: By reaching back to Maoist tactics of “rectification,” the Chinese leader is signaling that control over the gun requires a state of perpetual cleansi...

item.title: Syrian Army Crosses New Threshold With Advances in Kurdish Regions
item.description: The advance of the Syrian Army into Kurdish regions could put nearly all the country under one authority. But it ends a dream of autonomy for ethnic K...

item.title: User Issues With X Appear to Have Resolved
item.description: The social media site was the subject of user complaints early Monday.
